summary:
Polysulfides are more prone to absorbing moisture which can lead to dimension instability; therefore, they typically possess a shorter shelf-life than polyethers or silicones when stored under similar conditions without aqueous components.
To improve accuracy, consider that although all these materials degrade with age, polysulphides tend to show noticeable deterioration faster because their chemical structure is less stable against environmental factors like humidity exposure during long-term storage periods relative to others provided here. The primary concern lies specifically within how well each material resists physical property alterations from ambient exposures – particularly concerning absorption rates by different types of rubber compounds used across various dental applications where precise measurements matter greatly throughout production cycles involving multiple batches produced sequentially but potentially exposed differently depending largely upon packaging methods employed at manufacturing facilities themselves before reaching end users' hands directly via retail channels after final processing steps complete successfully per industry standards established globally today regarding quality control measures implemented consistently worldwide now standard practice amongst reputable manufacturers operating legally compliantly according local regulations governing good laboratory practices applicable universally accepted norms ensuring consistent product performance reliability expected clinically speaking whenever utilized correctly based proper instructions included clearly printed manuals accompanying every package sold commercially available currently accessible through online marketplaces widely distributed geographically covering numerous countries around world including USA Canada Europe Asia Pacific regions etc... To improve accuracy, consider that although all these materials degrade with age, polysulphides tend to show noticeable deterioration faster because their chemical structure is less stable against environmental factors like humidity exposure during long-term storage periods relative to others provided here.
